title: Isn't It, Don't You? — Tag Questions
description: Tag questions: the little checkers at the end of a sentence. 'It's hot, isn't it?' 'You like sushi, don't you?' Rules and rhythm.
summary_pt_br: Question tags em inglês: a perguntinha no fim da frase ('isn't it?', 'don't you?', 'aren't they?'). Regra: afirmação pede tag NEGATIVA, negação pede tag POSITIVA. Auxiliar espelha o verbo principal.

scenes.text_en:
Zara tilted her head. 'The coffee is cold, ISN'T IT?'
'You enjoy surfing, DON'T YOU?'
'They haven't arrived, HAVE THEY?'
'You aren't tired, ARE YOU?'
Four sentences, four tags. Flip the polarity, match the auxiliary.
scenes.text_pt:
Zara inclinou a cabeça. 'O café está frio, NÃO ESTÁ?'
'Você gosta de surf, NÃO GOSTA?'
'Eles não chegaram, CHEGARAM?'
'Você não está cansado, ESTÁ?'
Quatro frases, quatro tags. Inverte a polaridade, espelha o auxiliar.

scenes.title: Two rules for tag questions
scenes.body_en: RULE 1 — Flip the polarity: positive statement → NEGATIVE tag. Negative statement → POSITIVE tag. 'She's happy, ISN'T she?' / 'She isn't happy, IS she?' RULE 2 — Match the auxiliary. 'She IS' → 'isn't she?'. 'They HAVE gone' → 'haven't they?'. 'You LIKE it' (no auxiliary) → use DO: 'don't you?'.
scenes.body_pt: Duas regras: (1) Inverter polaridade — afirmativa pede tag negativa, e vice-versa. (2) Espelhar o auxiliar do verbo principal. Se não tem auxiliar, use do/does/did.

examples.pt: A gente deveria ir, não deveria?

exercise.question: Complete: 'Maya is smart, ___ she?'
exercise.hint_pt_br: Afirmativa pede tag negativa. is → isn't she.

exercise.question: 'They don't eat meat, ___?'
exercise.hint_pt_br: Negativa pede tag positiva. don't → do they.

exercise.question: Complete: 'You've been to Rio, ___ you?' (present perfect)
exercise.explanation: 've been = have been → tag uses HAVE. Positive → negative = haven't you.

turns.pt: Você gosta de cappuccino, não gosta?

turns.en: I do. You don't drink coffee, do you?
turns.pt: Gosto. Você não toma café, toma?

exercise.explanation: Five tag patterns. Notice the polarity flip every time.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Cinco tags. Sempre inverte a polaridade.

exercise.question: Complete: "Yes, it is really ___."
exercise.explanation: The full phrase is: "Yes, it is really beautiful."

exercise.question: Pick the best response. ('Sim, sou professor lá.')
options: Yes, I do. I am a teacher there.
exercise.explanation: The correct response is: "Yes, I do. I am a teacher there."

scenes.title: Confirming details
scenes.pt_summary: Tag questions — isn't it, don't you, aren't they.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: The weather is nice today, isn't it?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: You work at Amazing School, don't you?
turns.pt: Você trabalha na Amazing School, né?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes, I do. I am a teacher there.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Mr. Kumar is very kind, isn't he?
turns.pt: O Mr. Kumar é muito gentil, né?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes, he is the nicest boss.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: The students are studying hard, aren't they?
turns.pt: Os alunos estão estudando muito, né?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: You don't have class on Fridays, do you?
turns.pt: Você não dá aula na sexta, né?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: No, I don't. Fridays are my day off.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Isn't It, Don't You? — Tag Questions". Tag questions: the little checkers at the end of a sentence. 'It's hot, isn't it?' 'You like sushi, don't you?' Rules and rhythm. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Isn't It, Don't You? — Tag Questions". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
Isn't It, Don't You? — Tag Questions
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'Isn't It, Don't You? — Tag Questions'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "Isn't It, Don't You? — Tag Questions".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.
